prompt_md: A reporting tool exports CSV today. Product confirms PDF and JSON exports are both coming soon, and a developer suggests preparing for the change now. The export is triggered from one place in the code, and several callers will eventually need the new formats. What is the best seam to introduce, and why?

decision_choices: Add per-format if/else branches directly inside the export trigger
Introduce an Exporter interface with a format(data) method and one implementation per format
Add a configuration flag that selects the export format per environment
Build a general plugin registry covering exports, limits, and notifications together
reason_choices: Because there is a clear axis of change with concrete named variants (PDF, JSON), and an interface lets new formats slot in without editing the callers
Because configuration flags are always cheaper than interfaces regardless of the kind of change
Because inline branches keep all logic in one file, which is always easier to maintain
Because a single broad plugin registry is the most elegant way to handle every possible future change at once
